 {"id":"git_safety_guard-jpfm","title":"Span Highlighting for Blocked Commands","description":"# Epic: Span Highlighting for Blocked Commands

## Priority Score: 820/1000 (Highest Priority)

## Background and Rationale

This feature addresses the number one user pain point with security tools: Why was my command blocked?

When DCG blocks a command, users currently see which pattern matched, but not exactly WHAT part of their command triggered the match. This leads to user frustration, perception of DCG as arbitrary, time wasted debugging, and reduced trust.

### Example of Proposed Output

When a destructive command is denied, show caret-style highlighting:

  command --dangerous-flag /sensitive/path
                           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
                           Path targeting system file

## Technical Approach

The match span data ALREADY EXISTS in DCG evaluation pipeline. PatternMatch captures match info and regex find() returns byte positions. We just need to surface this in the output.

## Implementation Steps

1. Extend EvaluationResult to carry match span information
2. Create terminal formatter for caret-style highlighting with ANSI colors  
3. Integrate into hook output (stderr warning on denial)
4. Add to dcg test output for consistency

## Key Files

- src/evaluator.rs - Ensure match spans captured
- src/hook.rs - Integrate highlighting into denial output
- src/cli.rs - Update dcg test output
- New: src/highlight.rs or extend src/trace.rs

## Success Criteria

- Denied commands show exactly which substring triggered the match
- Works correctly with multi-byte UTF-8 characters
- Works for compound commands (multiple matches shown)
- Colors disabled gracefully when not in TTY
- No performance regression

## Cross-Model Validation

Both Claude Opus 4.5 and comparison model rated this 820/1000. Strong consensus on value.","notes":"Keep dependency separation: Visual Explanations epic should not depend on span capture/highlighting tasks.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:03:51.357954793-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T00:16:42.362535647-05:00"}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-jpfm.1","title":"[Span] Ensure match spans captured for all match sources","description":"# Task: Ensure match spans captured for all match sources

## Background
`matched_span` exists in `EvaluationResult`, but not all match paths populate it consistently.
Span highlighting will be unreliable unless every match source provides a byte span
(or an explicit fallback) in terms of the original command string.

## Scope
Cover **all** match sources:
- Pack regex matches (safe/destructive patterns)
- Legacy patterns (if any remain in evaluator paths)
- Heredoc/AST matches (inline script detection)

## Approach
- Audit match creation in `src/evaluator.rs` and ensure each code path sets:
  - `matched_span` (byte offsets)
  - `matched_text_preview` (already present; keep for fallback)
- For pack regex matches, use `regex.find()` from the selected engine to capture start/end.
- For legacy patterns, capture span from the legacy regex if used.
- For AST/heredoc matches, map extracted script offsets back to the original command when possible
  (use extraction metadata / heredoc boundaries). If mapping is impossible, store a structured reason
  and keep `matched_text_preview` as the fallback.

## Files
- `src/evaluator.rs`
- `src/heredoc.rs` / `src/ast_matcher.rs` (mapping helpers, if needed)
- `src/trace.rs` (optional: include span mapping reason in trace)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every deny/warn match source sets a usable `matched_span`, or records a clear fallback reason.
- Span is based on **original command**, not just normalized/sanitized forms.
- No performance regression on the hot path.

## Notes
This task is a prerequisite for reliable span highlighting. Keep it independent of the
Visual Explanations epic.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-jpfm.2","title":"[Span] UTF-8 safe slicing + windowing for long commands","description":"# Task: UTF-8 safe slicing + windowing for long commands

## Background
Highlighting needs to work on arbitrary UTF-8 input. Naive slicing can panic
on non-boundary indices, and long commands can create unreadable output.

## Scope
- UTF-8 boundary-safe slicing around match spans
- Ellipsis/windowing for long commands (show match context)

## Approach
- Create a helper that:
  - Accepts `command`, `matched_span`, and `max_width`
  - Returns a **windowed** command string and adjusted caret span
  - Preserves valid UTF-8 boundaries
- Ensure the caret line aligns with the windowed output.
- Prefer no allocations on short commands.

## Files
- `src/hook.rs` (formatter helpers)
- Optional: new `src/highlight.rs` to keep formatting utilities isolated

## Acceptance Criteria
- No panics on multi-byte characters
- Long commands are truncated with `...` while keeping the match visible
- Caret span aligns with windowed output (TTY and non-TTY)

## Notes
This is a core utility for the formatter. Keep it small and testable.

-{"id":"git_safety_guard-jpfm.3","title":"[Span] Respect TTY/non-TTY for highlighting output","description":"# Task: Respect TTY/non-TTY for highlighting output

## Background
DCG already disables ANSI colors when stderr is not a TTY. Highlight output
must follow the same rule so non-TTY logs remain clean and machine-readable.

## Scope
- Ensure caret/highlight formatting is correct in both modes
- Avoid ANSI sequences when `stderr` is not a TTY or when `NO_COLOR` is set

## Approach
- Use existing color control in `hook::configure_colors()` and `colored::control`.
- Provide a non-color fallback format (same caret positioning, no ANSI).
- Add tests or assertions in `deny_output_tests` if feasible.

## Files
- `src/hook.rs`
- `src/logging.rs` (if formatting helpers live there)

## Acceptance Criteria
- TTY: colored output with caret highlighting
- non-TTY: plain output with caret highlighting, no ANSI escape codes
- `NO_COLOR` respected
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:15:40.704625444-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T00:15:40.704625444-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-jpfm.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jpfm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:15:40.705902509-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-jpfm.4","title":"[Span] Preserve highlight accuracy for normalized/sanitized commands","description":"# Task: Preserve highlight accuracy for normalized/sanitized commands

## Background
DCG normalizes commands (wrapper stripping) and sanitizes safe spans.
If matching uses the normalized/sanitized string, highlight spans can drift
from the original command shown to users.

## Scope
- Ensure highlighted spans refer to the **original** command text
- Handle cases where normalization changes offsets

## Approach
- Track mapping between original and normalized/sanitized command when matches
  are computed on modified strings.
- If mapping is complex, compute match on the original command when safe
  (e.g., re-run regex on original for span only).
- Document fallback behavior when mapping is impossible.

## Files
- `src/evaluator.rs`
- `src/normalize.rs`
- `src/context.rs` / `src/trace.rs` (if mapping metadata stored)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Highlighted spans point to correct substring in original command
- No regression in decision logic (matching stays as before)
- Edge cases documented (e.g., when only preview is available)

## Purpose

in original-command coordinates.

## Requirements
- Tests for pack regex span capture
- Tests for normalization/sanitization offset mapping
- Tests for heredoc/AST span mapping or fallback behavior

## Acceptance Criteria
- Unit tests cover multiple match sources
- Failures include detailed diagnostics of expected vs actual spans

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-jpfm.6","title":"[Span] Unit tests for highlight formatter (UTF-8 + windowing + TTY)","description":"# Task: Unit tests for highlight formatter (UTF-8 + windowing + TTY)

## Purpose
Ensure highlight formatting is correct across UTF-8 input, long commands, and
TTY/non-TTY output modes.

## Requirements
- UTF-8 boundary cases (multi-byte chars)
- Long command windowing with ellipsis
- Non-TTY output contains no ANSI escapes

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests validate caret alignment and output length
- Tests fail with clear diffs when formatting shifts

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-jpfm.7","title":"[Span] E2E tests for hook output highlighting + verbose logs","description":"# Task: E2E tests for hook output highlighting + verbose logs

## Purpose
Ensure end-to-end hook output includes span highlighting with detailed logging
so regressions are obvious.

## Requirements
- Extend `scripts/e2e_test.sh` (or add new E2E script) to cover:
  - A blocked command with highlighting
  - Non-TTY mode (no ANSI)
  - Long command windowing
- Include verbose logs on failure to show full output

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E tests validate caret line positioning
- Logs include full stderr output for debugging

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-jt0","title":"[PACK] dns.cloudflare - Cloudflare DNS Pack","description":"# Cloudflare DNS Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for Cloudflare DNS operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### Wrangler CLI
- `wrangler dns-records delete`
- Zone purge operations

### API
- `curl -X DELETE api.cloudflare.com/.../dns_records/`
- `curl -X DELETE api.cloudflare.com/.../zones/`

### Terraform
- `terraform destroy` on cloudflare_record resources

## Safe Patterns
- `wrangler dns-records list`
- `curl -X GET` to Cloudflare API
- `wrangler whoami`

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: dns.* (git_safety_guard-x3g)
- Keywords: wrangler, cloudflare
- Note: Zone deletion affects all records

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] DNS record deletion blocked
- [ ] Zone deletion blocked
- [ ] API patterns detected
- [ ] Tests cover common Cloudflare operations","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:33:48.996879217-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T19:20:17.966286832-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T19:20:17.966286832-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented dns.cloudflare pack + registry + e2e tests","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-jt0","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:38.681210508-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-jt0","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:38.724429275-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-jt0","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:38.770402474-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-jt0","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:38.813135817-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-jt0","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:38.855065759-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-jt0","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:38.899342757-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-jt0","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:11.459747934-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-jt0","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:11.50597559-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-qyav","title":"[BUG][P1] pack_aware_quick_reject misses rm when args are /tmp or quoted ","description":"Regression: golden rm parity test shows allow for commands that should be denied (e.g. rm -r -f "/foo"). Suspect pack_aware_quick_reject_with_normalized fails to detect 'rm' keyword in executable spans when targets are /tmp or quoted , causing Step 4 quick-reject in evaluator to allow early. Fix quick-reject keyword gating to be conservative for rm/gits command words and add unit tests covering /tmp + quoted TMPDIR variants.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-11T05:18:30.416258064-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T06:04:36.386761667-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T06:04:36.386761667-05:00","close_reason":"Fix quick-reject + normalization for rm /tmp and quoted TMPDIR variants; added regression tests; full gates + e2e green."}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-r85z","title":"Preserve env assignments with command substitution","description":"strip_env currently skips env assignments even when they include backticks or  command substitution, which can hide destructive commands inside the assignment. Detect inline code in assignment tokens and stop skipping them; add regression test.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-12T12:43:00.958592452-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T12:44:41.048502315-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T12:44:41.048502315-05:00","close_reason":"Completed"}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-r97e","title":"Visual Explanations for Pattern Blocks","description":"# Epic: Visual Explanations for Pattern Blocks

## Priority Score: 750/1000 (Second Highest)

## Background and Rationale

This feature complements span highlighting by answering the deeper question: WHY does this pattern exist?

Users need to understand not just WHAT triggered the block, but WHY it matters. This transforms DCG from an obstacle into a teacher, building trust and helping users make informed decisions about allowlisting.

## Example Output

When showing a denial, include contextual explanation:

  Pattern: git.reset-hard
  Why: git reset --hard destroys uncommitted changes permanently.
       There is no way to recover work that has not been committed.
  
  Suggestion: Use git stash to save changes first, or git reset --soft
              to preserve changes in the working directory.

## Technical Approach

1. Add optional explanation field to DestructivePattern struct
2. Write explanations for all core patterns (approximately 30-40 patterns)
3. Create suggestion templates for common remediation steps
4. Integrate into hook denial output alongside span highlighting
5. Surface in dcg explain output

## Key Files

- src/packs/mod.rs - Add explanation field to DestructivePattern
- src/packs/core/*.rs - Add explanations to each pattern
- src/hook.rs - Display explanations in denial output
- src/trace.rs - Include in explain output

## Content Strategy

Explanations should include:
- What the command does (destructive action)
- Why it is dangerous (consequences)
- What could go wrong (scenarios)
- Safe alternatives (suggestions)

## Success Criteria

- Every core pattern has an explanation
- Explanations are concise but informative (2-4 sentences)
- At least one safe alternative suggested per pattern
- Documentation links where applicable

## Synergy with Epic 1

This works together with span highlighting - users see WHAT matched and understand WHY it matters. The combination is more powerful than either alone.","notes":"All packs must receive explanations; tasks under this epic partition the workload but do not narrow scope. Epic is not complete until explanations exist for core + all non-core packs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:04:33.031740999-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T00:26:21.925415787-05:00"}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-r97e.1","title":"[Explain] Explanation content style guide","description":"# Task: Explanation content style guide

## Purpose
Provide a consistent writing standard for explanations so tone, length, and
structure are predictable and professional.

## Requirements
- Short, neutral, factual language
- Avoid scaremongering or moralizing
- 2–4 sentences max unless complexity requires more
- Include consequence + safer alternative (when applicable)
- Prefer deterministic guidance over subjective phrasing

## Deliverables
- A short style guide in `docs/explanations.md` (or similar)
- Examples of **good vs bad** explanation text
- Recommended structure template

## Acceptance Criteria
- Guide is concise and usable by anyone editing pack patterns
- Example explanations cover git + filesystem + one non-core pack

## Notes
This guide is a dependency for writing large volumes of explanation text.

-{"id":"git_safety_guard-r97e.2","title":"[Explain] Fallback explanation for patterns without explicit text","description":"# Task: Fallback explanation for patterns without explicit text

## Purpose
Not all patterns will have bespoke explanations immediately. Provide a safe,
clear fallback to avoid empty explanation sections and keep UX consistent.

## Requirements
- Fallback should mention the matched pack + pattern name
- Explain that the command matched a destructive pattern and point to
  `dcg explain` or docs for details
- Must be neutral and concise

## Implementation
- Add fallback formatting in hook/CLI output when explanation is missing
- Expose fallback in explain JSON output for consistent tooling

## Acceptance Criteria
- No empty explanation sections in any output
- Fallback never blocks output rendering
- Works with both TTY and non-TTY formats

+{"id":"git_safety_guard-r97e.1","title":"[Explain] Explanation content style guide","description":"# Task: Explanation content style guide

## Purpose
Provide a consistent writing standard for explanations so tone, length, and
structure are predictable and professional.

## Requirements
- Short, neutral, factual language
- Avoid scaremongering or moralizing
- 2–4 sentences max unless complexity requires more
- Include consequence + safer alternative (when applicable)
- Prefer deterministic guidance over subjective phrasing

## Deliverables
- A short style guide in `docs/explanations.md` (or similar)
- Examples of **good vs bad** explanation text
- Recommended structure template

## Acceptance Criteria
- Guide is concise and usable by anyone editing pack patterns
- Example explanations cover git + filesystem + one non-core pack

## Notes
This guide is a dependency for writing large volumes of explanation text.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-r97e.3","title":"[Explain] Make explanation optional + preserve backward compatibility","description":"# Task: Make explanation optional + preserve backward compatibility

## Purpose
Ensure adding explanations does **not** break existing packs or call sites.
Patterns should function normally even without an explanation field.

## Requirements
- Explanation field is optional in `DestructivePattern`
- Default behavior: no compile/runtime failure if missing
- Output layer uses fallback explanation when missing

## Implementation
- Update `DestructivePattern` to carry `Option<&'static str>` explanation
- Update constructors/macros to allow absence
- Ensure serialization/tracing handles None cleanly

## Acceptance Criteria
- All existing packs compile without modification
- Explanation output renders even when field missing (via fallback task)

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-r97e.4","title":"[Explain] Write explanations for remaining (long-tail) packs","description":"# Task: Write explanations for remaining non-core packs (Batch B)

## Purpose
Complete explanation coverage by adding text for **all remaining** non-core packs
not covered in Batch A. This is required for full epic completion.

## Packs to Cover
Everything not handled by:
- core.git / core.filesystem
- non-core Batch A task

Examples include:
- monitoring.*
- messaging.*
- cdn.*
- platform.*
- email.*
- featureflags.*
- loadbalancer.*
- dns.*
- secrets.*
- storage.*
- remote.*
- apigateway.*
- cicd.*
- payment.*
- search.*
- system.* (if not covered elsewhere)

## Requirements
- Use the explanation style guide
- Include at least one safer alternative or mitigation tip
- Keep explanations short and neutral

## Acceptance Criteria
- All remaining packs have explanations for destructive rules
- No pack left without explanation text

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-r97e.5","title":"[Explain] Unit tests for explanation rendering + fallback + JSON","description":"# Task: Unit tests for explanation rendering + fallback + JSON

## Purpose
Guarantee explanations render correctly across hook/CLI/JSON outputs and that
fallback text is used when explicit explanations are missing.

## Requirements
- Unit tests for explanation formatting in hook output
- Tests verifying fallback usage when explanation is None
- Tests for explain JSON fields + schema version

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests cover both TTY and non-TTY formatting paths
- JSON output includes explanation fields when present
- Fallback is stable and non-empty when missing
